applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile with mode=default
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile to the entire model forward pass. Inductor fuses ReLU operations into preceding convolutions and uses optimized Triton MM kernels for linear layers. This eliminated ~12% elementwise overhead and selected better GEMM configs via autotuning.

attempted_but_failed.name: Custom Triton Linear+ReLU fusion
attempted_but_failed.reason: Custom Triton kernel was slower than hipBLAS (6.14ms vs 3.46ms)
attempted_but_failed.error: Kernel correctness passed but performance regressed significantly

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile on custom Triton conv kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton convolution kernels fail to legalize on ROCm
attempted_but_failed.error: failed to legalize operation 'ttg.async_copy_global_to_local' that was explicitly marked illegal

notes: VGG16 is dominated by convolution operations (78.6% of GPU time)
MIOpen assembly kernels are already highly optimized for MI355X
torch.compile(mode='default') successfully fuses ReLU into convolutions
Inductor autotune selects optimized Triton MM kernels that are faster than default
Custom Triton kernels for linear layers were slower than Inductor's choices

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 4 produced no output, same as trials 2 and 3. The agent keeps crashing. Need extremely explicit step-by-step instructions with defensive coding. Best so far is 60.70 from trial 1's torch.compile
